data.title: Veritone dips on $75M equity offering
data.description:
• Veritone (NASDAQ:VERI) announced a $75 million registered direct offering, selling about 12.86 million shares at $5.83 per share.
  • The deal, led by Needham & Company, is expected to close around October 17, 2025, pending customary conditions.
  • Veritone plans to use the proceeds for working capital, debt repayment, and general corporate purposes.
  • VERI --4.6% premarket to $8.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Salesforce, Google expand partnership to increase focus on AI, expand Gemini access
data.description:
Salesforce (NYSE:CRM) and Alphabet's Google (NASDAQ:GOOGL) (NASDAQ:GOOG) announced an expansion of their existing partnership on Thursday to focus on artificial intelligence, specifically bringing the search giant's Gemini AI models to the former's Agentforce 360 Platform.

Salesforce shares rose more than 6% in premarket trading amid optimism surrounding its annual Dreamforce event, while Alphabet shares rose 0.6%.

As part of the deal, Google's Gemini models will come to more of its Workspace software suite and work with Agentforce 360. This allows sales and IT services to have access to Google's latest models, and Salesforce's Atlas reasoning engine, the companies said in a statement. 

The deal will also see Gemini come to Salesforce-owned Slack, via the work management software's search API. This is particularly noteworthy, given that OpenAI (OPENAI) recently unveiled AgentKit, aimed at making it easier for workflow automation to integrate into ChatGPT.

“In the enterprise environment, it’s imperative for AI agents to be highly capable and highly consistent, especially for critical use cases,” said Silvio Savarese, Chief Scientist at Salesforce, in a statement. “Together, we are setting a new standard for building the future of what’s possible in the Agentic Enterprise down to the model level.”

“The deeper integration of Google Workspace with Gemini and Salesforce has the power to fundamentally improve how teams get work done,” said George Kwon, VP of Product Management for Google Workspace Platform and Growth. “This integration unifies critical business information with powerful AI assistance, helping teams across sales, customer service, and IT make smarter decisions and streamline their work with critical information, all from the tools they use every day.”

On Wednesday, Salesforce said it expects revenue by fiscal 2030 to hit $60B, above analysts' estimates.

data.title: Cosmos Health regains compliance with Nasdaq’s listing rule
data.description:
• Cosmos Health (NASDAQ:COSM) has regained compliance with Nasdaq’s $1.00 minimum bid price per share listing rule.
  • In the notice, Nasdaq stated that for the last 18 consecutive business days, from September 22, 2025, to October 15, 2025, the closing bid price of the company’s common stock has been at or above $1.00 per share.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Thermo Fisher taps Microsoft-backed OpenAI to speed up innovation
data.description:
Thermo Fisher Scientific (NYSE:TMO) on Thursday announced a collaboration with OpenAI (OPENAI) to leverage the Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT)-backed tech startup’s AI capabilities to accelerate its scientific innovation.

As part of the deal, the Waltham, Massachusetts-based life sciences company will integrate OpenAI APIs (Application Programming Interfaces) into its key business functions, such as product development, service delivery, and customer engagement.

The collaboration is expected to enhance the efficiency of multiple operational areas of Thermo Fisher (NYSE:TMO), including its Accelerator Drug Development solution and PPD clinical research business.

“This collaboration will help to improve the speed and success of drug development, enabling customers to get medicines to patients faster and more cost-effectively,” the company said.

“With OpenAI, we’re further embedding AI across our operations, products, and services,” CEO Marc Casper added. “Together, we’re building an ecosystem that accelerates scientific breakthroughs and delivers impact for customers, patients, and society.”

data.title: Brixmor Property CEO goes on medical leave
data.description:
Brixmor Property Group (NYSE:BRX) CEO James M. Taylor, Jr., is taking a temporary medical leave of absence as of Oct. 16, 2025, the company said on Thursday. The statement didn't provide any details on its CEO's medical condition or indicate any expected return date.

President and Chief Operating Officer Brian T. Finnegan will serve as interim CEO until Taylor returns.

data.title: Alibaba's massive bet on artificial intelligence beginning to pay off - report
data.description:
Alibaba’s (NYSE:BABA) multi-billion dollar investment in artificial intelligence is beginning to pay off for the Chinese e-commerce giant, defying critics that said it will take years for any company to see the benefits from their massive AI budgets.

In September, Alibaba (NYSE:BABA) said it would increase its current $53B budget for AI and cloud infrastructure, reflecting the company’s confidence in its usefulness to lure in shoppers by personalizing search results to enhanced virtual try-ons. According to comments from Alibaba’s e-commerce business quoted by CNBC, the company is already seeing a 12% increase in returns on advertising spending.

“It’s very rare to see double-digit changes,” Kaifu Zhang said through a translator, and predicts that the company’s embrace of AI will result in a “very significant” positive impact on Alibaba’s gross merchandise volume (GMV) during this year’s Singles Day shopping event, beginning November 11.

During the company’s most recent earnings call, Alibaba’s CEO assured investors that the company would need to made investments in e-commerce and AI on an “historical scale” to capitalize on an unprecedented time in the industry.

“Looking ahead, Alibaba Group has 2 historic opportunities, to build a technology platform centered on AI + Cloud and to create a comprehensive shopping and daily life services consumption platform. We will invest at scale to capture the opportunities. This also marks a new entrepreneurial chapter for the company after 26 years,” Yongming Wu said to analysts on the call.

“With a strong balance sheet, operating cash flow and business momentum, we are well positioned to support these investments, drive sustainable growth and strengthen core capabilities that would define Alibaba's future,” he added.

data.title: Sadot Group regains compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price rule
data.description:
Sadot Group (NASDAQ:SDOT) said on Thursday that the food supply chain company has regained compliance with Nasdaq's minimum bid price requirement.

Shares were -19.43% pre-market to $6.22.

In September, the company had implemented a 1-for-10 reverse stock split to regain compliance with Nasdaq's $1.00 minimum bid price rule.

data.title: NextCure and Simcere expand SIM0505 trial to the U.S.
data.description:
• NextCure (NASDAQ:NXTC) and Simcere Zaiming on Thursday said that the first patient in the U.S. has been dosed with SIM0505 in the ongoing Phase 1 trial (NCT06792552), which is evaluating safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics, and efficacy in patients with advanced solid tumors.
  • Multi-regional trial to accelerate time to proof-of-concept data in 1H 2026.
  • SIM0505 is a novel antibody drug conjugate directed to cadherin-6 (CDH6 ADC), featuring a proprietary topoisomerase 1 inhibitor (TOPOi) payload, designed for broad anti-tumor activity, high systemic clearance, and an improved potential therapeutic window.
  • NextCure has now expanded the ongoing Phase 1 dose escalation study, which was initiated in China, by enrolling patient((s)) in the U.S. into a mid-tier dose level while dose escalation is currently advancing in China.
  • NextCure acquired an exclusive global license, excluding Greater China, for SIM0505 from Simcere Zaiming.

data.title: DIH Holding announces 1-for-25 reverse stock split; shares down
data.description:
• DIH Holding US (NASDAQ:DHAI) will implement a 1-for-25 reverse stock split of its Class A Common Stock effective at market open on October 20, 2025.
  • The reverse split reduces the outstanding share count from ~52.3M to 2.1M.
  • There is no change to the par value or total authorized shares; ownership percentages and voting power remain largely unchanged.
  • DHAI shares down 23.8% premarket on Thursday.

data.title: NIO faces scrutiny after one of the world's largest sovereign wealth funds files a securities fraud lawsuit
data.description:
NIO (NYSE:NIO) is on watch on Thursday after Singapore sovereign wealth fund GIC filed a major lawsuit against the Chinese electric vehicle company, accusing it of inflating revenue by over $600 million through deceptive accounting methods related to its battery-leasing operations.

GIC was established in 1981 to manage Singapore’s foreign reserves with a long-term focus on achieving returns above global inflation over a 20-year horizon. The fund currently manages an estimated $850 billion in assets, which makes it one of the largest sovereign wealth funds globally and gives it a powerful voice. 

The lawsuit, which was filed in the U.S. District Court for the Southern District of New York, claims that NIO (NYSE:NIO), CEO William Li, and former CFO Feng Wei violated U.S. securities laws by issuing false and misleading statements about the company's  financials.

The complaint centers on Weineng Battery Asset Company, a battery-leasing affiliate established in 2020 with NIO's (NIO) suppliers and financial partners, including CATL and Guotai Junan International Holdings. Plaintiff GIC argues that NIO (NIO) improperly recognized immediate revenue from selling leased EV batteries to Weineng, even though customers made payments monthly under battery-subscription plans. Since Weineng was allegedly controlled by NIO (NIO), the lawsuit indicates the automaker should have consolidated its financial results instead of recognizing hundreds of millions of dollars in advance revenue. 

GIC claims it bought roughly 54.5 million NIO (NIO) shares during the alleged misstatement period and suffered "tremendous losses" when the truth emerged and the stock slumped.

Shares of NIO (NIO) fell 9% in Hong Kong trading. The U.S.-traded ADRs showed a 6.9% decline in the premarket session to $6.35. NIO (NIO) traded at a 2025 high of $8.02 earlier in the month. XPeng (XPEV) and Li Auto (LI) showed modest declines in the early session.
